title: When I use podman compose up to pull the image on my Windows system, it always shows "downloading" when only a few MB are left. I have configured a proxy, but this shouldn't have any impact.
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.Run wsl --shutdown.
2.Start Podman machine with podman machine start.
3.Navigate to the directory where the docker-compose.yml file is located.
4.Execute podman compose up


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

When I use podman pull nginx, it is able to pull the image correctly

When using podman compose up, it often gets stuck a few megabytes before completion, continuously showing "pulling." Occasionally, it succeeds after several attempts
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/60cd4d99-fa65-48f2-a827-39c9a12c5dfa)
Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Please provide a reproducer, and fill out the issue template fully. Also, please copy paste results rather than posting screenshots. Is this only an issue with podman-compose, or does running a podman command do the same thing?
please provide what @ashley-cui has recommended.  Also relevant would be if the image can be successfully pulled without compose (i.e. `podman pull <image>`)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Unable to reproduce, closing. Please feel free to re-open or file a new issue if you have a reproducer.
